architecture_gap: The current AAS runtime still needs a stable end-state architecture that can run self-improvement cycles without uncontrolled drift.
data_model_changes: Add convergence-aware cycle metrics, pressure-test traces, adversarial review traces, and freeze-state metadata.
governance_implications: Council deliberation now includes convergence and freeze decisions.
invention_concept: Strengthen registry-driven task routing, council replay, and adaptive execution scoring as the primary coordination surface.
migration_strategy: Promote only the strongest, pressure-tested, adversarial-reviewed architecture into the next CSSM version.
problem_statement: Pending architecture pressure remains on: Apply merged delta for Cycle 100 Meta-Merge Architecture, Apply selected architecture for Cycle 101 Converged Meta-Architecture.
proposed_architecture_change: Strengthen registry-driven task routing, council replay, and adaptive execution scoring as the primary coordination surface.
protocol_implications: The PAEE loop becomes bounded by convergence and explicit finalization rather than running indefinitely.
security_implications: Pressure and adversarial traces become explicit inputs to architecture selection.
title: Cycle 102 Registry-Governed Autonomy
